items.briefing:
The Australian Government has launched its Horizon 2 action plan under the national cyber security strategy, signalling continued investment in protecting Australians online — worth being aware of as your organisation may see updated guidance or requirements flowing from this. Separately, Scamwatch is warning about food delivery scams targeting Australians, where scammers impersonate platforms like Uber Eats or DoorDash to steal payment details or personal information.

A new AI tool called Claude Fable 5 is also raising security concerns, as experts warn it could be misused to craft more convincing phishing or social engineering attacks. Although phishing volumes are reportedly down, the risk remains high as attacks become more targeted and harder to spot.

**Practical tip:** If you receive an unexpected message about a food delivery or account issue, go directly to the official app or website rather than clicking any links in the message.

featured.title: Government launches Horizon 2 action plan for Australia’s cyber security strategy
featured.quote: Australia's cyber security strategy enters phase two with government-backed national action plan.
